content: I only really pulled one Rememberence char, and it's because she buffs my favorite character in the game
yeah, my favorites rn are Saber, Hotaru, and Phainon--not remembrance
I actively dislike her for fucking over my girl Rappa

That and I like Gundam, and Kamen Rider.
Rappa is one I dislike outright.
I have nothing against firefly as a character
I just hate how Taylor made Daliah is for her
And her main buffs that would help my girl do well don't work because of how specific they are
Daliah was made that way because she was made to buff firefly
Rather than break as a whole
just wait for them to make a break support that bails Rappa and Boothill out of prison
I just don't like how her being popular sucked away buffs from characters that needed them more
Could perfectly support Rappas main issues
Hotaru genuinely could not perform in PF and struggled immensely even at E2 in endgame prior to her buffs.
later half of 3.x was BRUTAL to her
But they're locked around Dahlia needing to use them conditionally
I'm not saying she didn't need buffs
But buffing ONLY her when Daliah could have universally buffed all other break DPSs would have been so easy
She did not need a condition that makes her ONLY Regen energy when a FIRE character implants waekness

then hate Hoyo's greed, not Hotaru. 

Hoyo buffed the one that sold the best of the three break main dps 5 stars. 

That happened to be Hotaru. 

Rappa debuted in the least popular patch we've had in a good long while. 

Boothill didn't sell well enough due to being more single target focused, and the favor at the time favored AOE/Blast. It still does, because Hoyo doesn't care much about Hunt.
I still don't like Firefly, but that's fair.
Also Rappa's design was not very popular for a good chunk of players, despite her obvious power.
I don't care about her design. I liked her Story
Her gameplay is also fun as shit when it gets going

even her story didn't go over well, many players urging people to simply skip it

I'm not arguing it's the best in the game
That's why I like her story
I'm not hating on Rappa, but explaining why nobody liked her.
rather, why the majority of players did not.
Hoyo saw her as a failure, so I don't think they're gonna bother buffing her back into relevancy.
She's still a Galaxy Ranger tho
so prep your pulls and keep your wallet close by if you care about her
because she''ll probably get an Expy at some point.

Finding good references for 13th century French noble and cavalry attire is harder than you'd expect
Doing research so I can make a Castlevania OC
I have a lot ideas I fuck with enough to consider doing a fangame
So did you know the French concept of lucky 13 is way older than unlucky 13 as a concept?
No, I did not know that
The 12 months are based on the twelve holy apostles
13 as an unlucky number comes from the last supper
In fact the dinner table was the only time the French considered 13 unlucky
Because of said associations with the last supper
Friday the 13th isn't a singular significant date, rather it's bad because Friday was the day Jesus was crucified and the 13th apostle is responsible for the last supper
So it's something of a social solar eclipse
That makes a lot of sense
My idea is Dracula gathers 12 unholy apostles to make a 13th month of total solar eclipse
And the MC, Treize(13) Belmont is the uninvited 13th guest who will bring it all down
I've gotten very positive responses so far
So I'm feeling very good about this concept
Since I'm a sucker for hunting a number of "big targets"
The protag is an arrogant ass hat blessed with incredible luck and a sharp intellect
His immense wealth comes from winning the lottery (a very real French thing at the time)
Dragula needs him all along to complete the thing
He's also very good at horse riding (due to both the association with horseshoes being lucky as well as the fact that horses were a sign of enormous status and wealth)
And he doesn't even know it
He dresses like nobility purely to spite them
I'm working on a manga first chapter for a short series, myself. But this is very much a 'practice' series rather than a magnum opus.
Also yes he is required for the spell to be cast
He fights the twelve apostles and their deaths charge the curse
your offer to me, I extend to you~
definitely will be taking that up after I draw out the final draft of ch 1
The idea being they are going to be sacrificed either way and so his job is to gain power from them when they're being sacrificed so he can stop Dracula himself from unleashing the curse
This is probably more thought than any CV game ever put into its setup but idc
Does he use a whip as a signature weapon
I fucking love themes and such
Or is it some bullshit like a lance
The vampire killer will start in normal whip form
And slowly transition to the more based design as he gets stronger
He also uses the whip as reigns
Themes and such is the best part of writing
The general gist of it is the whip is a permanent and evolving main weapon
Can he summon a horse at will
The weapons scattered through the castle would be secondary weapons
Rather than him having spells or transformations I'm going to have him summon monsters he subjugates
And probably some kind of satanic bird
And some kind of underwater creature as well
The ninth apostle looking as Treze pulls a fucking lance out his ass after just wielding a whip seconds ago:
Will the other main bosses be like
I assume their powers/designs will also take after that
Somewhat but there's not really enough to take from them in that regard
So really inspiration will need to come from multiple sources
I'm just saying in a general sense
Did you know 13 is also a Norse thing?
Loki was the uninvited 13th guest who directly caused the death of Baldr
So the twelve apostles would probably be themed after the twelve Norse gods
What's stopping you from making french Norse biblical apostles
I'm saying if I don't like them enough I'll find something else to draw inspiration from
Lazarus better be coolest motherfucker ever
Mostly because CV doesn't usually have a ton of vampires aside from Drac
And I don't feel like going against that
But yeah tldr I'm getting very French with it
The Loki thing fits even better

It all fits into place so cleanly~

Erm there was a post here about all the reruns

You have five routes to getting Mouchette, two of which needs you to pay

1. You could dump a lot of money _right now_  on pure silver and that eventually would unlock an arc 2 selector. That'd cost a lot, but it is guaranteed right now...
2. You could buy the Saya free-pulls banner's offer (1980 silver) then wait until 6th June - this would get you a Rebirth Core (full game selector) 
3. You can wait until the last week of these one-day banners and save pulls for it to guarantee her. She'll be up about the 4th June, but you'll need to save about 270 pulls to absolutely guarantee her I think (90 pulls guarantees her or her weapon, you can lose to her weapon twice I think)
4. You can try and get her from the arc 2 Kaleido banner right now - this is a good investment for most accounts right now anyway but the chances of getting Mouchette specifically are low
5. You can wait for her banner, which _probably_ re-runs before the end of the year
